items.title: Barcelona in Spaanse beker op bezoek bij Real Madrid-beul
items.summary: Voor Segunda División-club Albacete wacht opnieuw een schitterend affiche in de Copa del Rey. Na de enorme stunt tegen Real Madrid , wacht nu een confrontatie met Barcelona.

items.title: Marokko-schlemiel biedt excuses aan: 'Deze wond geneest niet makkelijk'
items.summary: Brahim Díaz is door het stof gegaan na zijn penaltymisser in de finale van de Afrika Cup . Door de mislukte panenka namens Marokko draaide de finale uit op verlenging, waarin Senegal aan het langste eind trok.

items.title: Frank to face media amid uncertain Spurs future
items.summary: Thomas Frank will face the media on Monday afternoon even though key figures at Tottenham are weighing up his future.

items.title: Conte makes subtle dig at Amorim over Højlund
items.summary: Napoli head coach Antonio Conte has appeared to aim a dig at former Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim.

items.title: Miami complete Allende transfer from Celta Vigo
items.summary: Inter Miami officially completed the permanent transfer of Tadeo Allende, signing the attacking player from LaLiga's Celta Vigo through the 2030 MLS season.

items.title: Heerenveen blijft strijdbaar: 'Niet zo dat onze doelstelling nu verandert'
items.summary: SC Heerenveen heeft voor dit seizoen de ambitie om Europees voetbal te halen, maar verloor zondag met 0-2 van FC Groningen, dat op de ranglijst nu zeven punten meer heeft. Een flinke teleurstelling voor de Friezen, maar het verandert niets aan de plannen.

items.title: In welk shirt zien we Cryscencio Summerville op het WK?
items.summary: Crysencio Summerville was belangrijk tijdens de derbyzege van West Ham United op Tottenham Hotspur en is om die reden door buitenlands voetbalkenner Süleyman Öztürk verkozen tot Nederlander van de Week.

items.title: Tottenham's Davies to have surgery on broken ankle
items.summary: Tottenham defender Ben Davies will have an operation on a broken ankle which could rule him out of Wales' World Cup play-off in March.

items.title: Is a Panenka ever the right choice for a penalty?
items.summary: Following Morocco's last-minute spot-kick miss in the Africa Cup of Nations final, BBC Sports asks if a Panenka is ever the right choice for a penalty.

items.title: Mbappé adviseert kritisch Bernabéu: 'Fluit dan het hele team uit'
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé heeft zich op een bijzondere manier uitgesproken over de aanhoudende fluitconcerten in Estadio Santiago Bernabéu. De superster van Real Madrid ziet dat spelers als Vinícius Junior en Jude Bellingham worden aangepakt door aanwezige fans, daar is de Fransman het niet mee eens.

items.title: Tickets, travel and Trump: How the 2026 World Cup is shaping up six months from the final
items.summary: The champion will be decided on 19 July in New Jersey. Here’s a rundown of the issues that will shape the tournament as it comes to North America We’re only six months from the biggest single sporting occasion in the world. On 19 July in East Rutherford, New Jersey, the men’s World Cup final will kick off and a champion will be crowned (although it will be hard to top the last one). The final will be more than a coronation (or confirmation, if Argentina repeat as champions). It will also be a culmination of six weeks of near non-stop soccer played across three countries, four time zones, and 16 cities. It’s likely that conclusions will already be drawn at that point on how the whole tournament fared. But for now, at this semi-convenient milestone, it’s worth taking stock of where we are six months out. Continue reading...

items.title: Wilson set to stay at West Ham despite exit talks
items.summary: West Ham block plans for striker Callum Wilson to leave the club this month after putting talks over his contract termination on hold.

items.title: Man United stun rivals City; Senegal win AFCON amid controversy; Barcelona lose; more
items.summary: What a weekend! Man United stunned their rivals City in a fiery derby, Senegal won AFCON amid controversial scenes, Thomas Frank is on the brink as Spurs boss and Barcelona somehow lost at Real Sociedad.

items.title: Chaotic AFCON final an embarrassment to soccer
items.summary: Fan clashes, VAR controversies, a missed penalty -- the AFCON final had it all. But not in a good way.

items.title: Morant inspired by 'energetic' European fans as NBA expansion talks continue
items.summary: Ja Morant returned to the NBA court in dazzling style in London on Sunday and the Memphis Grizzlies guard believes any player would embrace the delights of playing in a European atmosphere.

items.title: Haas become first team to reveal all-new 2026 F1 car
items.summary: Haas have become the first team to reveal images of their 2026 car as they launched the VF-26 for F1's new rules era.

items.title: Djokovic begins quest for 11th Australian Open title with impressive win
items.summary: Novak Djokovic opened his quest for an 11th Australian Open title and record-breaking 25th Grand Slam with an impressive first-round display in Melbourne on Monday.

items.title: Dalot 'would have been given red card in Europe'
items.summary: Manchester United's Diogo Dalot was not sent off against Manchester City after his foul on Jeremy Doku was "glancing" contact.

items.title: Hearn: Fury still in his prime for boxing return
items.summary: Tyson Fury is still in his "prime" and his return to boxing is great for the sport, says Eddie Hearn.

items.title: ‘Who on earth have we just signed?’: Donyell Malen makes instant impact for Roma | Nicky Bandini
items.summary: Gian Piero Gasperini is clearly a fan of the on-loan Aston Villa forward who shone in their 2-0 victory at Torino Was it even a real quote, or only an approximation, a convenient lead-in to columns such as this? After Donyell Malen put the ball in the net for the second time in the first half-hour of his Roma debut, a member of his new team’s coaching staff was reportedly heard asking: “ma chi abbiamo preso?” – who on earth have we just signed? Nobody would clarify who said this, and frankly it did not matter. The phrase was now canon, repeated in commentary and churned across the oceans of online news aggregation. It resonated because Roma’s supporters were asking the same question of a player who arrived from Aston Villa two days before. Continue reading...
